content: I'm so excited to start learning Spanish. I've always been fascinated by the language and culture.
That's great! What inspired you to start learning Spanish?
I'm actually planning to move to Taiwan in a few months, and I know that many Spanish-speaking people live there. I want to be able to communicate with them in their native language.
That makes sense. Taiwan is a fascinating country. What do you do for work, if you don't mind me asking?
I'm a professional rock climber and guide. I love my job, but I'm looking forward to a change of pace in Taiwan.
That's a thrilling job! Do you have any hobbies or interests outside of work?
I'm really into origami and stand-up comedy. I love the art of folding paper and making people laugh.
That's a unique combination of interests! Have you ever performed stand-up comedy before?
Yeah, I've performed at a few open mics in the city. It's a rush, but it's also terrifying. I'm thinking of starting a comedy club in Taiwan when I move there.
That sounds like a great idea! Do you have any experience with running a business or event?
Actually, I used to run a small rock climbing gym. It was a lot of work, but it was also really rewarding. I had to learn about all the different types of climbing and how to teach people.
That's impressive! I've never met anyone who's run a rock climbing gym before. How many students did you have?
I had around 50 students, including some experienced climbers. It was a lot of fun, but also a lot of responsibility. I'm thinking of starting a new rock climbing gym in Taiwan when I move there.
That sounds like a great plan. You'll have to keep me updated on how it goes. When are you planning to move to Taiwan?
I'm planning to move on August 15th. I'm excited, but also a bit nervous. It's a big change, but I'm ready for it.
I'm sure you'll do great. Taiwan is a beautiful country with a rich culture. You'll have to try some of the local food, like beef noodle soup.
I've heard great things about the food in Taiwan. I'm definitely looking forward to trying some new dishes. Maybe I'll even learn how to make some of them myself.
That's a great idea! Cooking is a great way to learn about a new culture. I'm sure you'll have a lot of fun trying new foods and drinks in Taiwan.
